name: loguru-configurable

metadata.name: loguru_configurable
metadata.summary: Wrapper that adds an application_settings ConfigSection to loguru

documentation:
# loguru_configurable #

Wrapper that adds an [application_settings](https://github.com/StockwatchDev/application_settings) ConfigSection to
[loguru](https://github.com/Delgan/loguru), a library which aims to bring enjoyable logging in Python.

Also relies on [loguru-config](https://github.com/erezinman/loguru-config) and
[loguru-logging-intercept](https://github.com/MatthewScholefield/loguru-logging-intercept).

modules.name: loguru_configurable
documentation: Entry point of the loguru_configurable library, collects all exportable items and disables logging by default.

modules.name: loguru_configurable._version
documentation: Version module, current version

modules.name: loguru_configurable.config
documentation:
Module that defines a ConfigSection for loguru, uses loguru-config to configure loguru
and loguru-logging-intercept to re-route standard logging calls

functions.name: loguru_configurable.config.default_handlers
documentation: Returns a default handler field for LoguruConfigSection.handlers; equals the default handler of loguru

classes.name: loguru_configurable.config.LoguruLevel

class_variables.name: loguru_configurable.config.LoguruLevel.name
documentation: Name of the logging level

class_variables.name: loguru_configurable.config.LoguruLevel.no
documentation: The severity of the level

class_variables.name: loguru_configurable.config.LoguruLevel.color
documentation: The color markup of the level; defaults to ''

class_variables.name: loguru_configurable.config.LoguruLevel.icon
documentation: The icon of the level; defaults to ''
Defines a loguru log level

classes.name: loguru_configurable.config.PatcherProtocol
documentation: Protocol class for a function argument for loguru.patch

classes.name: loguru_configurable.config.LoguruConfigSection

methods.name: loguru_configurable.config.LoguruConfigSection.get_loguru_config
documentation:
Return a LoguruConfig instance initialized with the fields of self; so that one can do
`.parse().configure()

class_variables.name: loguru_configurable.config.LoguruConfigSection.inplace
documentation:
Whether modifications to the logger configuration should be made in-place. If False, a copy of the configuration
is made before modifications are made. Defaults to False.

class_variables.name: loguru_configurable.config.LoguruConfigSection.do_configure
documentation:
Whether to configure the logger after loading the configuration. If False, the configuration is loaded but
not applied to the logger. This is useful if you want to load the configuration and then modify the LoguruConfig
object before applying it to the logger. Defaults to False (which differs from loguru_config, but it needs to be
False, otherwise the default config will always be configured due to initialization of class variables).

class_variables.name: loguru_configurable.config.LoguruConfigSection.activation
documentation:
The activation configuration to be passed to `logger.add`. The sequence contains tuples of the form
`(logger_name, active)`, where `logger_name` is the name of the logger to activate, and `active` is a boolean
indicating whether the logger should be active or not; defaults to a list containing a single tuple to activate
the root logger.

class_variables.name: loguru_configurable.config.LoguruConfigSection.handlers
documentation:
List of handlers to use. Each handler is given as a dict with parameters that configure the handler.
The 'sink' parameter is mandatory and specifies where to send the formatted log messages. The handler
configurations are passed to `logger.add` as keyword arguments. See the examples
folder for examples. For more info on handler parameters refer to
[the logure docs](https://loguru.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api/logger.html#loguru._logger.Logger.add).
Defaults to the default handler configuration of loguru.

class_variables.name: loguru_configurable.config.LoguruConfigSection.levels
documentation: A list of loguru logging levels, to add to the standard levels; defaults to an empty list.

class_variables.name: loguru_configurable.config.LoguruConfigSection.extra
documentation: The default contents of the `extra` dictionary (without calling `logger.bind`); defaults to an empty dict.

class_variables.name: loguru_configurable.config.LoguruConfigSection.patcher
documentation:
Specifies the record-patcher parameter in `logger.configure` that functions like `logger.patch`;
post-init converts the string (if not empty) to a Callable[[loguru.Record], None]; defaults to ''.

class_variables.name: loguru_configurable.config.LoguruConfigSection.intercept
documentation: Whether to intercept calls to Python's standard `logging` module and route them to loguru; defaults to False.

class_variables.name: loguru_configurable.config.LoguruConfigSection.intercept_level
documentation:
This level of calls to standard logging and above will be intercepted and routed to loguru; defaults to 'DEBUG'.
This is only used if `intercept` is True. Valid values: 'DEBUG', 'WARNING', 'INFO', 'ERROR' and 'CRITICAL'.

class_variables.name: loguru_configurable.config.LoguruConfigSection.intercept_modules
documentation:
A list of modules to intercept calls from, in addition to the root logger (which is always intercepted if
intercept is True); defaults to an empty list.
ConfigSection for loguru-config
